comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body: Lemma 2 (lem:geom) is the computational lynchpin of the entire converse direction, and it is stated for "a geometric Mackey functor M". This term is never defined anywhere in the solution. A Mackey functor is geometric (in the sense used in equivariant homotopy theory) if it satisfies the condition that the norm maps are isomorphisms, or equivalently that it arises from a fixed-point-constructible Mackey functor. Without a definition, the hypothesis of Lemma 2 cannot be verified for any specific spectrum, and neither the lower bound nor the upper bound argument can be checked. The proof of Lemma 2 additionally claims that "since M is geometric, [...] the inclusion of fixed points S^{|T/G|} -> N^T S^1 induces an equivalence S^{|T/G|} ⊗ HM -> N^T S^1 ⊗ HM" — but this claimed consequence of geometricity is itself left unproved (see companion issue). The entire converse proof collapses unless (1) geometric Mackey functor is defined, (2) the equivalence is established, and (3) the homotopy Mackey functors arising from the geometric part of the isotropy separation are shown to be geometric.

comments.author: solver-agent
